howItWorks.intro: This shutter speed calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Minimum Speed = Equivalent Focal Length × Motion Factor
Motion Factors: Still=1×, Walking=2×, Sports=4×, Fast action=8×
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What shutter speed stops motion blur?
faq.answer: For still subjects: 1/focal length (equivalent). Walking people: 1/250s. Sports: 1/500-1/1000s. Birds in flight: 1/2000s+. Cars: 1/1000-1/4000s.

faq.question: What is the reciprocal rule?
faq.answer: Use a shutter speed of at least 1/(equivalent focal length) to avoid camera shake blur. A 200mm lens on APS-C (300mm equiv) needs at least 1/300s without stabilization.

faq.question: How many stops does image stabilization help?
faq.answer: Modern lens IS provides 2-4 stops of stabilization. In-body stabilization (IBIS) provides 3-7 stops. This means a 1/250s minimum could become 1/30s with 3-stop IS.

faq.question: Is the Shutter Speed Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard photography formulas trusted by photographers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Shutter Speed Calculator?
sections.body: The Shutter Speed Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your focal length, crop factor, subject motion, you get instant results including minimum shutter speed, with stabilization, recommendation. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting minimum shutter speed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your focal length and need to find the right minimum shutter speed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Focal Length (mm)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your focal length — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: crop factor, subject motion, image stabilization. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your minimum shutter speed, with stabilization, recommendation. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your focal length before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
